title: Spiral Matrix
description:
Given an `m x n` matrix, return all elements of the matrix in spiral order (clockwise from the top-left).

**Traversal Order:** Right along top row, Down along right column, Left along bottom row, Up along left column — then repeat for the inner layer.

**Boundary Shrinking:** Maintain four boundaries: `top`, `bottom`, `left`, `right`. After traversing each direction, shrink the corresponding boundary inward and verify boundaries are still valid.

**Constraints:**
- `1 <= m, n <= 10`
- `-100 <= matrix[i][j] <= 100`
editorial: Use boundaries top=0, bottom=m-1, left=0, right=n-1. Loop while top<=bottom and left<=right. Traverse: left->right along top (top++), top->bottom along right (right--), right->left along bottom if top<=bottom (bottom--), bottom->top along left if left<=right (left++). O(m*n) time.

rexHints.level1: Think of the matrix as concentric rings. You peel off the outermost ring, then move inward.
rexHints.level2: Track four boundaries: top, bottom, left, right. Each pass goes right along top, down along right, left along bottom, up along left — then shrink each boundary inward.
rexHints.level3: top=0,bottom=m-1,left=0,right=n-1. While top<=bottom && left<=right: traverse top row left->right, top++; traverse right col top->bottom, right--; if top<=bottom traverse bottom row right->left, bottom--; if left<=right traverse left col bottom->top, left++.
